title: Distance Vector Routing
subtitle: Bellman-Ford Algorithm in C
description: Implementation of Bellman-Ford algorithm for routing

theory.overview: Distance Vector Routing is like asking your neighbors for directions. You don't know the whole map, but you know your neighbors, and they know theirs. By sharing what you know, everyone eventually figures out the shortest path to every destination.

key_concepts.definition: A table stored in each router that keeps track of the shortest known distance to every other node.
The algorithm used to calculate the shortest paths in a weighted graph. It works by repeatedly relaxing the path costs.
The state where all routers have the correct, optimal routing tables and no further updates are needed.

protocol_flow: Step 1: Each router starts knowing only its direct neighbor costs
Step 2: Routers share their distance vectors with neighbors
Step 3: Each router updates its table if a shorter path is found
Step 4: Process repeats until no more updates occur (convergence)

steps.title: Define Data Structures
steps.explanation: We define a struct `node` to represent each router's routing table. It stores the shortest distance to every other node (`dist`) and the next hop to get there (`from`). We create an array `rt` to hold the routing table for up to 10 routers.

steps.title: Initialize Cost Matrix
steps.explanation: We read the initial link costs between all pairs of routers into `costmat`. The distance from a router to itself is set to 0. We also initialize each router's routing table with these direct link costs.

steps.title: Bellman-Ford Relaxation
steps.explanation: This is the core of the algorithm. We use three nested loops to check every possible path. For every router `i` and destination `j`, we check if going through an intermediate router `k` is cheaper than the currently known path. If it is, we update the distance and the next hop.

steps.title: Convergence Check
steps.explanation: The `do-while` loop continues as long as `count != 0`. `count` tracks how many updates were made in the current iteration. When no updates are made (count is 0), it means the routing tables have converged to the optimal paths.

exercises.hint: What is the cost of traveling from router i to router k directly?
